items.headline: U.S. Equities Close Higher as Investors Embrace Risk and Eye Geopolitical Developments
items.summary: U.S. stock markets concluded the week with gains, as investors shifted focus past policy concerns and re-engaged with risk assets. The uplift was notably driven by renewed interest in high-growth technology and space-related companies. Optimism surrounding a potential U.S.-Iran peace agreement also contributed to improved market sentiment.

items.headline: SpaceX Soars in Landmark IPO Debut
items.summary: SpaceX made its highly anticipated public debut on the Nasdaq, with shares jumping 19.22% and closing near $161. The IPO raised $75 billion, marking the largest public offering in history and valuing the company above $2 trillion.

items.headline: Financials Show Strength
items.summary: Goldman Sachs was among the top performers, contributing to the Dow's overall gains.

items.headline: Nike Dips Amid Market Rally
items.summary: Nike experienced a notable decline among the Dow components.

items.headline: Tech Giant Sees Slight Pullback
items.summary: Apple shares registered a decrease as other tech names saw renewed interest.

items.headline: Bitcoin Slips as Crypto Lags Risk-On Mood
items.summary: Bitcoin saw a slight decline, lagging the broader risk-on sentiment in the equity markets.

items.headline: U.S.-Iran Peace Agreement Hopes Buoy Markets, Oil Prices Fall
items.summary: Optimism surrounding a potential U.S.-Iran peace agreement is bolstering investor sentiment, fueled by expectations of reduced geopolitical risks and increased stability in energy markets. President Trump indicated a deal signing today, although Iran clarified the date is not yet final. Brent crude oil prices dropped 3.4%.

items.headline: Technology and Space Sectors Lead Gains; Nasdaq-100 Rebalance Announced
items.summary: High-growth technology and space-related names were key drivers of the market's ascent. The Nasdaq-100 Index announced its June 2026 rebalance, effective June 22, adding Astera Labs ($ALAB), CoreWeave ($CRWV), Nebius Group ($NBIS), Rocket Lab ($RKLB), and Teradyne ($TER).

items.headline: Inflation Concerns Persist Amid 'Trump Bull Market' and Fed Rate Discussions
items.summary: While major indices reached new record highs in the 'Trump Bull Market,' inflation remains a serious concern for Wall Street. President Trump continues to advocate for lower interest rates, with the Federal Open Market Committee's (FOMC) current target range of 3.5% to 3.75% being above his preferred level.

items.headline: Upcoming Earnings Reports on Investor Radar
items.summary: The week of June 15 will see earnings reports from several major companies, including Jabil Inc. ($JBL), CarMax, Inc. ($KMX), Accenture plc ($ACN), and The Kroger Co. ($KR). Oracle ($ORCL) reported earnings above expectations at $2.11 USD.
